sections.heading: Installation
sections.text: Download the .mcaddon from CurseForge. Double-click the file to automatically import the addon into Minecraft. Create or open a world and go to Behavior Packs in world settings. Activate the Behavior Pack. The resource pack is linked automatically. These steps apply to all JM Addons.

sections.heading: Updating
sections.text: The update process differs slightly depending on whether you're running a Realm or a local/singleplayer world. Follow the steps for your setup below.

sections.heading: Realms
sections.text: Deactivate the BP and RP from your Realm, then save. Go to Settings, Storage and remove the old behavior pack and resource pack. Close Minecraft completely. Download the new .mcaddon file and open it. Verify the new version number.

sections.heading: Non-Realm Worlds
sections.text: Go to Settings, Storage and remove the old behavior pack and resource pack. Close Minecraft completely. Download the new .mcaddon file and open it. Activate the behavior pack and make sure the resource pack activates too. Always remove old packs before importing new versions.

sections.heading: Keeping your data
sections.text: Updates preserve your world data. Scoreboards, tags, dynamic properties, and any in-game state created by the addon stay intact after updating. Removing the old packs from storage only removes the addon files; your world's saved data is untouched.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text: Addon not appearing in Behavior Packs? Make sure Minecraft was fully closed before importing. Resource pack not loading? It should activate automatically; check Active Packs. Features not working after update? Close and reopen the world.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: Tag Tool is a lightweight addon that lets operators add and remove tags on players without needing cheats on. No fumbling with the /tag command, no losing achievements. Just a clean in-game UI you open by using the item. Focused entirely on tag management.

sections.heading: Usage
sections.text: How to use the Tag Tool: opening the UI, adding tags, removing tags, in-game help, and protected tags.

sections.heading: Opening the UI
sections.text: Hold the Tag Tool item and use it (right-click, or the interact button on mobile/controller) to open the main menu. Operators receive the item automatically on first join. It can also be crafted with a paper and a gold ingot together (shapeless).

sections.heading: Adding a tag
sections.text: From the main menu, choose My Tags to manage your own tags, or Target Player to pick another online player. Select Add Tag, type a name into the text field (max 256 characters), and confirm. The tag is applied immediately.

sections.heading: Removing a tag
sections.text: From the tag manager screen, select Remove Tag to see a list of all tags currently on that player. Select one to remove it immediately.

sections.heading: In-game help
sections.text: The main menu includes a How to Use button that opens a built-in help screen covering all features. It's always up to date with the installed version.

sections.heading: Protected tags
sections.text: Tags beginning with sb_, scoreboard, or jm_ are hidden from the Tag Tool UI and cannot be added or removed through it. This prevents accidental modification of internal addon or scoreboard state.

sections.heading: Commands
sections.text: Tag Tool is UI-first and doesn't add custom slash commands. Standard Bedrock /tag commands still work alongside it. Native commands like /tag @e[type=cow] add my_custom_tag still work as expected.

sections.heading: Compatibility
sections.text: Tag Tool uses a minimal footprint to avoid conflicts with other packs. Adds one custom item (jm_tt:tag_tool) with a crafting recipe, no custom blocks. No global scoreboard entries. No dynamic properties used, so zero namespace footprint.

sections.heading: Operator access
sections.text: Tag Tool checks operator status in three stages: command permission level, player permission level, and tag fallback (op, operator, or admin tags). Works on all devices.

sections.heading: Changelog
sections.text: v1.0.0 - Initial release. Multi-screen Script API UI for add/remove/view. Player tag management for your own tags or any online player. Full Script API 2.5.0 compatibility.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: Admin Tools is a complete in-game admin panel for Minecraft Bedrock servers and realms. Full control over player moderation, ticketing, land claims, ranks, teleportation, and server settings through in-game menus. Runs on Script API with zero experimental features. Cheats off, achievements on. v2.1 adds the Elevator Block, co-owner trust level, sub-sub-claims, Quick Run, Customize Menu, owner-controlled server-wide menu visibility, reworked action log, pinned warps, mount carry on teleport, hologram limit raised to 100, and more.

sections.heading: Getting Started
sections.text: How to install and set up Admin Tools. Covers installation, first launch, and all custom items and recipes.

sections.heading: Installation
sections.text: Download the .mcaddon file from CurseForge. Open it to auto-import. Enable both Admin Tools BP and RP. Operators receive the Admin Tool on first join. The first admin becomes the Owner. You can change the owner with /jm_at:transferowner or /jm_at:resetowner.

sections.heading: First Launch
sections.text: Operators receive the Admin Tool and Player Tool automatically. All players receive the Player Tool on first join. Use the Admin Tool to open the Admin Panel, and the Player Tool to open the Player Menu. Resource pack includes button styling subpack options.

sections.heading: Items & Recipes
sections.text: Seven custom items: Admin Tool (staff only, opens Admin Panel), Player Tool (all players, opens Player Menu), Claim Tool (select corners for land claims), Claim Viewer (see claim boundaries), Hologram Tool (admin, floating text), Chest Sorter (sort chest contents), Elevator Block (4x Iron Ingot + Piston diamond pattern, vertical teleport between stacked pads). All recipes viewable in-game through the Info menus.

sections.heading: Elevator Block
sections.text: Craftable block that teleports players vertically. Jump on a pad to go up, sneak to go down. Stack two or more pads in the same column. Maximum distance between pads is 128 blocks. If no pad is found within range, nothing happens. Respects claim protection. Recipe: 4x Iron Ingot + Piston (shaped, diamond pattern).

sections.heading: Roles & Permissions
sections.text: Admin Tools uses a four-tier permission system: Player, Moderator, Admin, and Owner. Understanding these tiers is key to knowing who can do what on your server.

sections.heading: Permission Tiers
sections.text: Player: all players, can use Player Tool. Moderator: assigned by admins, sees limited Moderator Panel. Admin: operator permissions or admin tag, full Admin Panel access. Owner: first admin to open Admin Tool, with exclusive abilities like gamemode change and clearing the admin log.

sections.heading: Moderator Privileges
sections.text: Configurable globally and per-moderator. Enabled by default: Tickets, Warn, Kick, Temp Ban (max 24h), Freeze, Heal/Feed, TP to Player, Spy Camera, View Inventory, Punishment History. Disabled by default: Server Controls, Config Editor, Warps, Broadcasts, Gamemode, Rank Panel, and more.

sections.heading: Owner Menu Visibility
sections.text: The server owner can hide optional top-level buttons from the Admin Tool main page server-wide, affecting every admin's view. Disabling a player-facing feature in the Config Editor now also hides its button from every player's Player Tool menu automatically.

sections.heading: Player Features
sections.text: Features available to all players through the Player Tool: tickets, homes, warps, teleport, deathback, player stats.

sections.heading: Player Tool Menu
sections.text: Hold the Player Tool and use it to open the Player Menu. Craftable with Paper + Redstone (shaped, vertical) or given on first join. Contains buttons for: Tickets & Warnings, Homes, Warps, Teleport, Deathback, My Stats, and Info.

sections.heading: Tickets & Warnings
sections.text: Submit tickets by category with configurable cooldowns and limits. Ticket lifecycle: Open, Claimed, Resolved, Closed. View active warnings, acknowledge them, and submit appeals. Resolved tickets auto-close once viewed.

sections.heading: Homes
sections.text: Save locations as named home points, then teleport back anytime. Up to configurable max (default 3, max 20). Each home has a color icon. Update Home button moves the saved location to your current position without deleting it. Teleporting to a home brings your mount (horse, mule, camel, llama, pig, strider) and nearby parrots along. Also applies to warps, TPA, summon, and dimension-lock ejection.

sections.heading: Warps
sections.text: Server-wide teleport points created by admins. Players can browse and teleport to any public warp. Warps show dimension icons. Staff-only warps hidden from regular players. Teleportation to locked dimensions is blocked. Warps can be pinned to always appear at the top of the list. Both pinned and unpinned warps can be reordered.

sections.heading: Teleport
sections.text: Send teleport requests to other online players. Requests expire after 60 seconds. Both players must stay still during the countdown; moving cancels it. Last Location button sends you back to your location before your last teleport. Countdown now cleanly cancels if the requesting player moves or disconnects. Mount carry applies.

sections.heading: Deathback
sections.text: Return to the location where you last died. Tracks death point automatically. Tool restore is a three-way setting: Never, Always, or Only if held at death. Warning shown if death location is unsafe. Configurable in Config Editor.

sections.heading: Player Stats
sections.text: Tracked automatically for every player: playtime, mob kills, player kills, deaths, blocks mined, blocks placed, and distance traveled. Visible through My Stats and to admins through player profiles.

sections.heading: Land Claims
sections.text: Players and admins can claim and protect land using the Claim Tool (select corners) and the Claim Viewer (see boundaries). Claims protect builds from griefing with configurable protections. Three-level hierarchy: parent claim, sub-claim, sub-sub-claim. Everything managed through in-game menus.

sections.heading: Creating Claims
sections.text: Hold the Claim Tool. Crouch + Hit a block to set Corner 1. Crouch + Interact to set Corner 2. Open Claim Tool normally to access Claims menu, then Confirm Pending Claim. Auto-detects overlapping or bordering claims. Claims extend from bedrock to sky automatically.

sections.heading: Claim Viewer
sections.text: Hold the Claim Viewer in your offhand, and the action bar displays info about the claim you're standing in. Crouch while holding it to show boundary particles in a 20-block range. Particles are only visible to you. Current Claim button in any claims list view instantly selects the claim you are standing inside.

sections.heading: Protections
sections.text: 13 protection toggles per claim: Blocks, Containers, Entities, PvP, Interactions, Item Use, Explosions, Hostile Mobs, Crop Trampling, Fire Spread, Ender Pearls, Eject Players, Allow Vehicles (exempts minecarts, boats, and addon vehicles; off by default). Mob Whitelist lets specific mob types spawn inside the claim even with hostile mob protection on. Container break protection fix: breaking a container now correctly blocked. Fire spread no longer extinguishes fires on netherrack, soul sand, or soul soil.

sections.heading: Members & Trusted
sections.text: Four member roles: Visitor (enter only), Member (interact with doors, buttons, levers), Builder (full build and interact), Co-owner (full management permissions, can create/delete sub-claims, cannot delete the root claim). Add members by name with optional expiring access. Trusted Ranks grant access by server rank.

sections.heading: Sub-Claims
sections.text: Three-level hierarchy: parent claim, sub-claim, sub-sub-claim. Example: city claim, district sub-claim, individual house sub-sub-claim. Up to 10 sub-claims per claim. Each level has independent protections and member lists. Global claim limit pool is shared across all levels.

sections.heading: Expanding & Extending
sections.text: Expand: select a larger area encompassing the claim to grow it. Extend: select a bordering area to attach an extension (up to 2 per claim). Extensions share protections and members. Expanding an extended claim merges into one rectangle.

sections.heading: Budgets & Chunks
sections.text: Cost measured in chunks (16x16 = 256 blocks XZ area). Starting budget: 5 chunks. Earned: +1 per 60 min playtime. Max cap: 15 chunks. Admins can grant bonus chunks. Sub-claims don't cost extra. All settings configurable in Claims Config.

sections.heading: Admin Claims
sections.text: Server-owned protected areas that don't count against any player's budget. Ideal for protecting spawn, shops, arenas, and shared builds. Transfer Ownership, Force Delete, Edit Protections, and Adjust Height are available through every claim access path.

sections.heading: Claim Log
sections.text: Admin claim log overhauled in v2.1. Entries display as inline body text, 15 per page. Bracketed colour-coded event tags: [Member Added], [Deleted by Admin], [Transfer], and more. Filter by player name or action type. Clear Filters appears only when filters are active.

sections.heading: Admin Panel
sections.text: Central hub for server management. Open with the Admin Tool. Includes: Search, Tickets, Players, Control Panel, Commands, Warps, Broadcasts, Admin Log, Customize, and Info. Moderators see a filtered version based on privileges.

sections.heading: Tickets (Admin)
sections.text: View tickets across four tabs: Open, Claimed, Resolved, Closed. Actions: claim, reply, resolve, close, teleport to submission location, delete. Configure ticketing on/off, categories, cooldowns, limits, and auto-delete.

sections.heading: Player Management
sections.text: Browse players in three views: Online, Offline records, and Search by name. Select any player to open their profile and access moderation actions.

sections.heading: Player Profiles
sections.text: Each profile includes: View Tickets, Punishment History, Warnings (with appeals), Spy History, Admin Notes (up to 20), and Rank. Player stats (playtime, kills, deaths, blocks, distance) visible here. Admin home detail view is now an action form with Teleport, Delete, Back, and Close.

sections.heading: Actions & Moderation
sections.text: Kick, Temp Ban, Perm Ban, Unban, Warn, Freeze, Heal/Feed, TP to Player, Summon, Clear Inventory, Gamemode (no cheats needed), Assign/Remove Moderator, Make/Remove Admin. Warn threshold is configurable.

sections.heading: Spy Camera
sections.text: Silently orbit around a target player in spectator mode. Look around to orbit, walk to adjust distance, crouch to stop. Sessions are logged. Works on all platforms including consoles and Realms. Now uses spectator mode internally, fixing held item display issues and enchanted item save failures.

sections.heading: Inventory Viewer
sections.text: View a player's hotbar and armor slots. Ender chest contents available (snapshot taken when the player opens their ender chest).

sections.heading: Customize Menu
sections.text: Each admin can customize which buttons appear on their own Admin Tool main page. Hide buttons you rarely use, or promote sub-menu items as shortcuts on the main page. Changes only affect your own view.

sections.heading: Server Controls
sections.text: Server-level configuration and management tools: Config Editor, Item Watch, Ticket Settings, Dimension Locks, Server Controls, and Restore Holograms. Storage Health moved to the Action Log & Reports hub.

sections.heading: Control Panel
sections.text: Central server configuration hub. Contains Config Editor, Item Watch, Ticket Settings, Dimension Locks, Server Controls, and Restore Holograms. Storage Health moved to the Action Log & Reports hub in v2.1.

sections.heading: Server Lock & Whitelist
sections.text: Server Lock prevents non-staff from joining. Toggle via UI or /jm_at:lockserver. Whitelist allows only approved players (up to 200). Toggle via UI or /jm_at:whitelist.

sections.heading: Dimension Locks
sections.text: Lock any dimension instantly or with countdown announcements (5 min, 1 min, 30 sec). Players inside locked dimension sent to spawn. All teleportation to locked dimensions blocked. Mount carry applies on ejection.

sections.heading: Config Editor
sections.text: All addon settings in one place. Changes take effect immediately. Sub-sections: Homes, Teleport Settings, Deathback, Warn Flag Threshold, Gamemode Access, AFK Settings, Mod Privileges.

sections.heading: Item Watch
sections.text: Banned items are automatically removed from player inventories. Tracked items alert admins when a player holds more than the configured threshold. Persistent offline log of flagged events viewable under the Action Log & Reports hub.

sections.heading: Storage Health
sections.text: Monitors dynamic property storage usage. Now located under Admin Log, not the Control Panel. Limits: Claims 3000 (sub-claims and sub-sub-claims share this pool), Tickets 500, Admin log 800, Player registry 500 (eviction warning at 450). Automatic alerts on admin join when approaching capacity.

sections.heading: Ranks & Progression
sections.text: Create and manage player ranks with custom colored name tags, auto-promotion based on playtime, and bulk assignment tools.

sections.heading: Rank Database
sections.text: Create up to 100 named ranks with custom colored name tags. Each rank has an internal name (up to 20 chars) and display tag (up to 40 chars with Minecraft color/style codes). Add, edit, remove from Rank Manager.

sections.heading: Bulk Assign / Remove
sections.text: Assign a rank to many players at once (including offline players), or remove a rank from all or selected players.

sections.heading: Staff Tags
sections.text: Customize the default [Admin] and [Mod] name tags. Toggle per-staff custom rank display.

sections.heading: Playtime Ranks
sections.text: Automatically promote players based on playtime thresholds (up to 20 tiers). Optionally override manually assigned ranks. Bonus playtime feature to credit untracked time for players who played before the addon was installed.

sections.heading: Broadcasts & Logging
sections.text: Server-wide announcements and activity logging for moderation tracking. Logging hub includes Action Log, Rollcall, Staff Activity, Storage Health, and Item Watch Log.

sections.heading: Broadcasts
sections.text: Send one-time multi-line announcements to all players, or set up scheduled recurring broadcasts (up to 5 active at a time).

sections.heading: Action Log
sections.text: All moderation actions logged with timestamps and staff names. Entries use bracketed italic timestamps and colour-coded source tags [ADM], [PLR], [SYS]. PvP kills logged with PVP_KILL tag. Unified Filters button covers player, action type, and source in one modal. Clear Filters shown only when active. Clear Log button moved into the Action Log view. Silent eviction: no chat broadcast when old entries roll off. Rollcall and Staff Activity Back/Close buttons now have icons.

sections.heading: Saved Commands
sections.text: Save and run custom slash commands. Up to 50 per admin. Single-modal add/edit flow covers name, command, repeat toggle, and delay in one step. Delete button lives on the command detail page. One-shot commands with no delay now run immediately. Some commands may require cheats.

sections.heading: Quick Run
sections.text: New button on the Commands panel. Opens a single text field to run a command immediately without saving it to your list. Useful for one-off commands. Logged in the Action Log like any other admin command.

sections.heading: Holograms
sections.text: Floating text displays placed in the world. Admin-only using the Hologram Tool (Sign + Glow Ink Sac). Up to 100 holograms per world (raised from 50 in v2.1), 10 lines each, 60 chars per line. Supports Minecraft color codes. Holograms placed in v2.1 or later use jm_at:hologram_v2 with half-block entity height for better ceiling placement. Earlier holograms keep original height. Edit by interacting with Hologram Tool. Restore at Control Panel if despawned.

sections.heading: Chest Sorter
sections.text: Craftable by all players. Use the Chest Sorter item on any vanilla chest, trapped chest, copper chest, barrel, or shulker box to sort its contents alphabetically. Recipe: Iron Ingot + Chest + Iron Ingot (shaped, vertical).

sections.heading: AFK Detection
sections.text: Configurable idle detection with warning timer (default 2 min) and optional auto-kick (disabled by default, 10-120 min configurable). Staff can be exempted. AFK players see cycling subtitle reminders. Moving or interacting resets the timer.

sections.heading: Custom Commands
sections.text: Slash commands for quick access: /jm_at:transferowner, /jm_at:resetowner, /jm_at:freeze, /jm_at:unfreeze, /jm_at:warn, /jm_at:heal, /jm_at:feed, /jm_at:summonplayer, /jm_at:lockserver, /jm_at:whitelist, /jm_at:forceunfreeze, /jm_at:removemod, /jm_at:mobwhitelist <add|remove|list> <entity_type_id>. Also /scriptevent jm_at:resetData for full reset.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting & FAQ
sections.text: Common issues: Admin Tool not in inventory, check operator status and pack versions (v2.1). Change owner with /jm_at:transferowner. Achievements stay enabled, no cheats needed. Moderators cannot perm ban. Reset everything with /scriptevent jm_at:resetData (run twice). Holograms disappeared, restore from Control Panel. Storage full, check Admin Log > Storage Health and delete old tickets. Claims not working, check BP/RP versions and budget. Offline unbans now reliable; if a stale ban persists, unban again through the Admin Tool.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: Notice Board replaces wall-of-signs setups with one organized place for community posts. Players write announcements, trades, rules, and event notes from an in-game menu. Posts persist in dynamic properties across restarts. Achievement-safe, no experiments. Categories, expirations, comments, search, saved posts, archive, and per-board local mode.

sections.heading: Installation
sections.text: Both the Behavior Pack and the Resource Pack must be enabled, since block models live in the RP. Requires Minecraft Bedrock 1.21.100 or newer. No experimental toggles. Posts persist in world dynamic properties across restarts and pack updates.

sections.heading: Crafting Recipe
sections.text: Shaped recipe at any Crafting Table: top row 3 cobblestone, middle row log plank log, bottom row 3 logs. Outputs 1 Notice Board item. Found in the creative Items tab. Stacks to 16. Total ingredients: 3 cobblestone, 1 plank, 5 logs.

sections.heading: Placing the Board
sections.text: Multi-block structure that auto-builds when placed. Right-click the top of a block for a standing board (3 wide x 3 tall, 9 blocks). Right-click the side of a block facing N/S/E/W for a wall board (3 wide x 2 tall, 6 blocks). Standing boards auto-orient to face the player. Footprint must be clear or placement fails silently.

sections.heading: Breaking the Board
sections.text: Breaking any block of a notice board removes the entire structure and drops one Notice Board item. Posts, categories, pins, archive, templates, and activity log are preserved, since data is keyed to dimension and position. Replacing a board at the same coordinates restores the dataset. Use Reset Board to wipe data without breaking it.

sections.heading: Opening the Menu
sections.text: Right-click or long-press any block of a notice board to open the Main Menu UI. Closing forms has no effect on the world. The title bar shows the current scope: Notice Board (Global) or Notice Board (local label). Multiplayer-safe: concurrent writes are protected by a world-level lock that auto-clears in under 3 seconds.

sections.heading: Main Menu
sections.text: Categories at top, actions below. Action order: Search, Saved, Addon Info, Add Notice (players if allowed plus admins), Add Admin Notice (admins only), Board Controls (admins only). Hidden and admin-only categories are filtered for non-admins. Category buttons show an unread badge when new posts have been added since last viewed.

sections.heading: Search
sections.text: Modal text field for keywords. Multi-keyword AND search across post title, body, author name, and category name. Results sorted by most recently posted or edited. Tap any result to read; back returns to results. Search Again button on the results page.

sections.heading: Saved
sections.text: Personal bookmarks list. Up to 200 saved post IDs per player; older entries dropped FIFO. Looks up posts in both active and archive lists, so bookmarked posts stay accessible even after they expire.

sections.heading: Creating a Post
sections.text: Post composer fields: Category dropdown, Title (button name), 5 line text fields joined with newlines, Expiration dropdown (default 7d), Include Location toggle (default ON). Each line accepts literal \n for manual line breaks. Body capped at 1000 characters on save.

sections.heading: Expiration durations
sections.text: 8 expiration options: 12hr, 24hr, 48hr, 72hr, 7d (default), 14d, 30d, Infinite. Time is real-time, so posts expire even if no players are online. If admins disable infinite posts via Allow infinite posts, the Infinite option is rejected at submit time.

sections.heading: Templates
sections.text: Admins create templates under Board Controls Post Templates. When templates exist for the current scope, the post composer shows a Choose a template or start blank picker. Templates pre-fill title, category, and body. Players only see templates whose category they can post in.

sections.heading: Approval queue
sections.text: When the admin setting Require approval queue is ON, every player post is sent to the Pending Queue instead of going live. Admins review via Board Controls Pending Queue and choose Approve or Deny. Admin posts always go live immediately and skip the queue.

sections.heading: Reading a Post
sections.text: Post detail view shows title in bold, info line of category age author, full message text, optional location stamp, edited timestamp, and expiration countdown. Action buttons depend on permission and post state: Comments, Save/Unsave, Edit, Delete, Lock/Unlock, Pin/Unpin, Approve/Deny, Back.

sections.heading: Comments
sections.text: Each comment up to 30 characters. Each post can hold up to 20 comments total. 60-second cooldown between comments per player. You can delete your own; admins can delete any. Locked posts also lock comments. Comments can be disabled per-category or globally.

sections.heading: Categories
sections.text: 5 built-in categories: News, Trade, Rules, Miscellaneous, Admin Notices (hidden by default). Admin Notices always sorts to the bottom. Per-category flags: visible, admin-only posting, comments off, custom icon (21 to choose from), description text shown at the top of the category page.

sections.heading: Admin access
sections.text: A player is treated as a board admin if any are true: they are an Operator (/op name), their command permission level is GameDirectors or higher, or they have the admin tag. Use /tag player add admin or /tag player remove admin. The admin tag is a fallback for older Bedrock builds where OP detection isn't reliable.

sections.heading: Pinning posts
sections.text: Each scope (global feed and each local board) has 4 pin slots. From a post tap Pin and choose Slot 1-4. Pinned posts list first in their category. Pinning replaces whatever was in that slot. Pins are scope-wide but only display inside their own category.

sections.heading: Locking posts
sections.text: Locking prevents anyone (including the author) from editing the post. Comments on a locked post are also locked. Admins can still edit or delete a locked post. The post detail view shows the lock icon and Comments (Locked) label.

sections.heading: Board Controls
sections.text: Admin-only hub at Notice Board Board Controls. Buttons: Settings (Global/Local), Pending Queue (only when approval queue is ON), Manage Categories, Post Templates, Activity Log, Archive (only when archiving is ON), Reset Board (Defaults), Main Menu.

sections.heading: Settings
sections.text: Single ModalForm with toggles. Global-scope: Allow player posts (default ON), Allow infinite posts (ON), Require approval queue (OFF), Allow comments (ON), Enable post archiving (ON), Auto-delete archived notices after 14 days (ON). Local-scope: Board mode (Global by default), Local label (max 20 chars). Six of the eight settings apply globally to every board.

sections.heading: Manage Categories
sections.text: 8 actions: Add category, Rename, Set icon (21 icons), Set description, Toggle visibility, Lock posting (admin-only), Toggle comments, Remove category. Removed-category posts get reassigned to Misc. At least 1 category must remain. Local-board categories only exist on that board.

sections.heading: Post Templates
sections.text: Admin-only screen with Add template, Edit template, Remove template. Each template has a name, default category, and body text. When templates exist, the post composer shows a picker before the post form. Common uses: pre-formatted Trade Listings, Event templates, Rule announcements.

sections.heading: Activity Log
sections.text: Shows the last 20 activity entries for this scope, newest first. Tracks posts, edits, deletes, approvals/denials, pins/unpins, locks, comment activity, category changes, template changes, archive deletions. Capped at 50 stored entries internally. Has a Clear log button.

sections.heading: Archive
sections.text: Lists expired posts for the current scope, sorted by most recently expired. Only available when Enable post archiving is ON. Tap to view (read-only). Admins can permanently delete individual posts or use Delete all. Auto-delete sweeps archived posts older than 14 days. Archive caps at 250 entries per scope.

sections.heading: Reset Board
sections.text: Destructive: no undo, no backup. Local-mode reset clears this board's local posts, pins, pending, archive, templates, and category overrides; the board returns to Global mode. Global-mode reset wipes the entire global feed across all Global boards, resets settings to defaults, and resets the category list.

sections.heading: Global vs Local Mode
sections.text: Global mode (default): all Global boards share posts, pins, categories, archive, and templates. Local mode: each board has independent posts, pins, archive, pending, templates, and category overrides. Mode is set per-board under Board Controls Settings Board mode. Switching to Local does not copy global posts. Switching back hides local data but does not delete it.

sections.heading: Limits & Caps
sections.text: Active posts per scope 80, Pending 80, Archived 250, Activity log stored 50 displayed 20, Saved posts per player 200, Per-category posts per page 15, Pin slots per scope 4, Comment length 30 chars, Comments per post 20, Comment cooldown 60 seconds, Post body 1000 chars, Local label 20 chars, Per-player seen entries 250, Category icons 21. Database stored in dynamic properties with 32767-char single-key cap and 4-key chunked fallback.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting
sections.text: Most common issue: only one of BP and RP enabled. Both packs must be applied at v1.0.4. Add Notice missing means Allow player posts is OFF. Board Controls missing means you aren't an admin. Submitted message means the approval queue is on. Local-mode boards have their own categories. Posts can be auto-deleted by expiration or archive auto-delete. Busy/lock prompts auto-clear in 3 seconds. Debug command: /function jm_nb/debug or chat !nbdebug verbose.

sections.heading: Reference Tables
sections.text: 21 category icons: bed, bell, book_and_quill, bread, brick_block, bundle, campfire, chest, clock, command_block, compass, diamond_chesplate, diamond_hoe, knowledge_book, locations, nether, news, pin, sign, sword, trade. Default icon is pin. Comment system constants: 30 chars, 20 per post, 60s cooldown.

sections.heading: Compatibility
sections.text: Bedrock 1.21.100 or newer. Script API @minecraft/server 2.1.0 and @minecraft/server-ui 2.0.0. No experiments needed. Achievement-safe. Cheats not required. Designed for multiplayer with concurrent-write lock. Uses jm_nb namespace exclusively. Adds 15 custom block IDs (9 standing + 6 wall). Posts persist across restarts via dynamic properties.

sections.heading: Overview
sections.text: A full postal system for Bedrock worlds. Players craft mailboxes, place deposit boxes, and send items, sealed packages, or written letters to each other on a schedule or instantly. Designed for survival servers, realms, and shared worlds. Mail lives in world dynamic properties. Achievement safe, no experiments required.

sections.heading: Installation
sections.text: Both the Behavior Pack and the Resource Pack must be enabled. The mailbox and package block models live in the RP. Both packs ship in a single .mcaddon import. Requires Minecraft Bedrock 1.26.0 or newer. No experimental toggles required.

sections.heading: Getting Started
sections.text: Every player gets a Mail Delivery Guide on first join. The guide covers Info, How To, Recipes, and Admin Info. Admins get an extra Admin Controls button. All blocks and items are crafted at a standard crafting table.

sections.heading: The Guide Book
sections.text: Players receive a Mail Delivery Guide on first join. Use it to open a 4-section in-game reference. Can also be crafted from a single sheet of paper. Admins see an Admin Controls button inside the guide.

sections.heading: Crafting Recipes
sections.text: Mailbox on Post: Iron Ingot, Log, Cobblestone. Wall Mailbox: Iron Ingot, Paper. Mail Deposit Box: 5 Iron Ingots, Paper. Open Package: 8 Paper in a ring. Mail Delivery Guide: 1 Paper. The log type decides the post style for all 10 wood types.

sections.heading: Mailboxes
sections.text: The core of the system. Personal inbox for receiving mail, outbox for sending on a schedule, and the only block a recipient needs. 11 variants: 10 wood post types plus a wall mailbox.

sections.heading: Variants & Customization
sections.text: 11 mailbox variants: Spruce, Oak, Birch, Dark Oak, Acacia, Cherry, Jungle, Mangrove, Pale Oak posts, and a Wall Mailbox. Post mailboxes are 2 blocks tall. Dyeing with 16 dyes. Stripping post with an axe. 320 post textures total.

sections.heading: Ownership
sections.text: Placing a mailbox auto-claims it. Only the owner or an admin can open it. Breaking the mailbox clears the claim. Admins don't auto-claim on placement, they see a Claim or Grant form instead.

sections.heading: The Mailbox Menu
sections.text: Inbox, Outbox, Send Item in Hand, Write Letter, and Friends. Inbox and outbox hold up to 60 entries each. One entry equals one send action.

sections.heading: Mail Deposit Box
sections.text: Instant version of the mailbox. Anyone can use it, not owned by anyone. Send Item in Hand, Write & Send Letter, Send a Sealed Package. 2 blocks tall. Place in shared spaces like spawn, markets, trading halls.

sections.heading: Packages
sections.text: Physical blocks that hold items, get sealed into a carryable item, and then mailed. Open Package holds up to 54 stacks. Sealed Package keeps all contents and can be placed back as Open Package.

sections.heading: Open Package
sections.text: Holds up to 54 stacks. Deposit items one at a time or use Bulk Deposit. Optional recipient label. Block model swaps to closed when items are inside.

sections.heading: Sealed Package
sections.text: Breaking an Open Package with items produces a Sealed Package item. Keeps contents, recipient label, and retention timer. 7 day retention unused, 30 days in outbox or returned. Can be placed back as Open Package.

sections.heading: Letters
sections.text: Standalone text messages. Compose from Deposit Box (instant) or Mailbox Outbox (scheduled). Multi-recipient letters allowed by default. Items and packages are always single-recipient.

sections.heading: Friends List
sections.text: Personal friends list accessible from mailbox menu. Up to 60 entries per player, oldest dropped when full. Names auto-refresh from player registry.

sections.heading: Delivery Schedule
sections.text: 4 schedule modes: Noon (in-game), Hourly (real time), Every X in-game days (1-30), or Immediate. Deposit Box sends always fire instantly. Failed pickups return mail after 3 attempts or 60 in-game days.

sections.heading: Receiving Mail
sections.text: Players see a chat message and note chime when they join with waiting mail. Claim entries from inbox. Items go to inventory or drop at feet if full. Letters and sealed packages are claimed as items.

sections.heading: Admin Guide
sections.text: Admin panel accessed from Mail Delivery Guide. 5 sections: Settings, Item Blacklist, Audit Log, Mailbox Tools, and Broadcast Letter. Admin access via Operator or admin tag.

sections.heading: Admin Access
sections.text: Admin if Operator (/op) or has admin tag. Grant via /tag player add admin, revoke via /tag player remove admin.

sections.heading: Settings
sections.text: Enforce Item Blacklist, Audit Log Enabled, Allow multi-recipient letters, Max audit log entries (10-150), Mailbox delivery schedule, Day interval (1-30), Send cooldown (0-60 seconds). Admins always bypass cooldown.

sections.heading: Item Blacklist
sections.text: Blocks specific item IDs from being sent or packed. Add from inventory grid or by item ID. Some items blocked by default: containers, maps, addon items. Paginated list with Clear Blacklist button.

sections.heading: Audit Log
sections.text: Records admin actions and key events. Ring buffer capped at 10-150 entries (default 100). Paginated log with Clear Audit Log button.

sections.heading: Mailbox Tools
sections.text: View/delete any player's Inbox or Outbox. Purge Old Mail. Purge Unused Package Payloads. Inactive Mailboxes (60+ days). Destructive actions with no undo.

sections.heading: Broadcast Letter
sections.text: Send a single letter to every known mailbox owner. Useful for server-wide announcements. Recorded in audit log.

sections.heading: Limits & Caps
sections.text: Inbox 60 entries, Outbox 60 entries, Package 54 stacks, 3 failed pickups before return, 7 day sealed package retention (unused), 30 day retention (in outbox/returned), 60 friends per player, 10-150 audit log entries, 0-60 second send cooldown, 1-30 day interval.

sections.heading: Troubleshooting & FAQ
sections.text: Missing textures: enable both BP and RP. Can't open mailbox: only owner or admin can open. Mail never arrived: check delivery schedule, deposit box sends instantly. Package shows closed: cosmetic when items inside. Item blocked: on blacklist, admin can review. Send cooldown: default 3 seconds, admin configurable. Lost Guide Book: craft with 1 paper.

sections.heading: Compatibility
sections.text: Bedrock 1.26.0 or newer. Script API @minecraft/server 2.6.0 and @minecraft/server-ui 2.0.0. No experiments needed. Achievement-safe. Cheats not required. Designed for multiplayer. Uses jm_md namespace exclusively. Adds 22 custom block IDs. Persists via dynamic properties.
